title: Young North star hospitalised as Geelong run riot in bloodbath outing
subtitle: Geelong’s dominant start to the 2024 season reached destructive heights on Sunday as they tore North Melbourne to shreds at home.
content:
Geelong has continued its undefeated start to 2024 — and its ominous recent record against North Melbourne — defeating the Kangaroos 21.13 (139) to 10.4 (64) on Sunday afternoon at GMHBA Stadium.
The Cats have now won 12 consecutive games against North Melbourne in a streak that stretches back to Round 12, 2016. 
The Kangaroos, now 0-5, have now conceded at least 100 points in all five games they’ve played thus far this season. 
Superstar forward Jeremy Cameron starred for Chris Scott’s brigade, booting six goals to go along with 21 disposals, eight marks and 11 score involvements. 
Tyson Stengle, Gryan Miers, Ollie Henry, Brad Close and Tanner Bruhn each registered eight score involvements of their own. 
Geelong’s dominant victory moves it to second on the ladder behind GWS, with the pair of clubs now the only undefeated teams remaining in 2024. 
Geelong opted to rest Tom Hawkins and didn’t rush Patrick Dangerfield’s return — and it turns out they didn’t need their star veterans as they ran out 75-point winners. 
Without Hawkins in attack, Jeremy Cameron had a day out as he racked up a whopping 15 disposals, four goals and eight score involvements in the first half alone. 
“It’s already a day out,” Fox Footy’s Brad Johnson said of Cameron’s work rate at half time.
“That’s what sets him apart from everyone else in the competition. He runs (the Roos) into the ground.
“North Melbourne are on their haunches because of how powerful he is.”
Cameron Mooney described Cameron’s first half as “phenomenal”. 
“With (Hawkins) out, was he going to anchor himself to the goal square? The answer is no,” he said. 
“He’s playing his natural game, getting up the ground and running his opponent into the ground.
“We’ve seen it a couple of times now when North defenders just couldn’t run back with him.
“He’s run into an open goal on a couple of occasions.”
Cameron finished his afternoon with a stuffed stat sheet: 21 disposals, six goals and 11 score involvements. 
Brandan Parfitt was another to shine in a star’s absence as he took over in the midfield without Dangerfield.
Johnson said the looming selection headache was a good one for coach Chris Scott.
“The competition for spots at Geelong is going to become tight very quickly,” he said.
“That’s where it’s going to be tough for Parfitt, (Jack) Bowes… you just can’t play them all, unfortunately.”
Mooney said he was pleased to see plenty of talent coming through at the club.
“It’s taken 12 months for (their recruits) to settle into this team and this town, and we are just seeing the fruits of it in the first five weeks of this season.”
Geelong is now one of just two teams unbeaten this season, joining GWS at the top of the table.
North Melbourne young gun Colby McKercher lasted less than a quarter at the Cattery on Sunday after a heavy collision with Geelong star Jeremy Cameron.
McKercher had attacked the footy at half-back when he made contact with Cameron coming in the other direction.
While Cameron came away rubbing his hip, McKercher made his way to the bench and later was taken to hospital as a precaution.
Fox Footy’s Cameron Mooney described the collision as a fair bump.
“It really was a flush hit from Jeremy Cameron,” he said. 
“Two boys attacking the ball, (Cameron) turned his body at the right time and got his backside right into the ribcage.”
Commentator Dwayne Russell questioned if the turning of the body was the way to go given recent Match Review scrutiny.
“The turning of the body in contests is something that’s been discouraged a bit because you don’t want to put your backside into someone else’s head,” he said.
“To lead in front on.”
“It’s how we were all taught as kids,” Mooney responded.
“Does the AFL want to discourage the turning of your backside and go in head-first rather is the way they want you to go?” Russell questioned. 
Colby McKercher was subbed out of the game following this first quarter collision with Jeremy Cameron.
North Melbourne was given AFL assistance at the end of last season after another luckless year. 
But even with that help, the Roos remain winless in 2024 – with fears their year could be just as long as the last.
North started 2023 with back-to-back wins in promising signs under new coach Alastair Clarkson.
But Clarkson and the Roos haven’t been able to replicate that start.
“Tough year for the Kangaroos who are yet to have a win this season,” commentator Dwayne Russell said on Fox Footy.
To fully appreciate the chasm between these sides, consider that Bailey Scott has played more AFL games than Brad Close but after today Close will have played in 48 more wins.
“At least they started well last year (with two straight wins). It’s been a tough time.
“Guys like Eddie Ford playing today - 26 games for only one win … makes it tough for development.”
Three-time premiership winner Cameron Mooney forecast another “tough year” at Arden Street.
“It’s going to be a tough year as a key forward for North Melbourne.”
Debutant Tyler Sellers didn’t have a touch until late in the final term as he struggled to get near it, making just one tackle in three quarters of footy. 
Originally published as
en_title: Young North star hospitalised as Geelong run riot in bloodbath outing
en_content:
Geelong has continued its undefeated start to 2024 — and its ominous recent record against North Melbourne — defeating the Kangaroos 21.13 (139) to 10.4 (64) on Sunday afternoon at GMHBA Stadium.
The Cats have now won 12 consecutive games against North Melbourne in a streak that stretches back to Round 12, 2016. 
The Kangaroos, now 0-5, have now conceded at least 100 points in all five games they’ve played thus far this season. 
Superstar forward Jeremy Cameron starred for Chris Scott’s brigade, booting six goals to go along with 21 disposals, eight marks and 11 score involvements. 
Tyson Stengle, Gryan Miers, Ollie Henry, Brad Close and Tanner Bruhn each registered eight score involvements of their own. 
Geelong’s dominant victory moves it to second on the ladder behind GWS, with the pair of clubs now the only undefeated teams remaining in 2024. 
Geelong opted to rest Tom Hawkins and didn’t rush Patrick Dangerfield’s return — and it turns out they didn’t need their star veterans as they ran out 75-point winners. 
Without Hawkins in attack, Jeremy Cameron had a day out as he racked up a whopping 15 disposals, four goals and eight score involvements in the first half alone. 
“It’s already a day out,” Fox Footy’s Brad Johnson said of Cameron’s work rate at half time.
“That’s what sets him apart from everyone else in the competition. He runs (the Roos) into the ground.
“North Melbourne are on their haunches because of how powerful he is.”
Cameron Mooney described Cameron’s first half as “phenomenal”. 
“With (Hawkins) out, was he going to anchor himself to the goal square? The answer is no,” he said. 
“He’s playing his natural game, getting up the ground and running his opponent into the ground.
“We’ve seen it a couple of times now when North defenders just couldn’t run back with him.
“He’s run into an open goal on a couple of occasions.”
Cameron finished his afternoon with a stuffed stat sheet: 21 disposals, six goals and 11 score involvements. 
Brandan Parfitt was another to shine in a star’s absence as he took over in the midfield without Dangerfield.
Johnson said the looming selection headache was a good one for coach Chris Scott.
“The competition for spots at Geelong is going to become tight very quickly,” he said.
“That’s where it’s going to be tough for Parfitt, (Jack) Bowes… you just can’t play them all, unfortunately.”
Mooney said he was pleased to see plenty of talent coming through at the club.
“It’s taken 12 months for (their recruits) to settle into this team and this town, and we are just seeing the fruits of it in the first five weeks of this season.”
Geelong is now one of just two teams unbeaten this season, joining GWS at the top of the table.

title: ‘Greatest of all time’: Buzzerbeater KO, ‘absolute savage’ stun epic UFC 300 card
subtitle: A champ’s absolutely savage act has capped off an insane UFC 300 fight card that also witnessed the “greatest knockout of all time”.
content:
UFC 300 well and truly lived up to the hype. 
One of the most stacked cards in recent history produced a slew of insane knockouts at T-Mobile Arena in Las Vegas. 
Alex Pereira showed why his left hand is arguably the most dangerous weapon in UFC, knocking out Jamahal Hill in just over a minute to win their light heavyweight showdown.
Earlier, Max Holloway knocked out Justin Gaethje in the very last second of their fight in a victory that’s been called the “greatest knockout of all time”.
Alex Pereira has done it again, defeating Jamahal Hill a minute into their light heavyweight title fight in the main event.
Referee Herb Jones stepped in after Hill kicked Pereira with a low blow but the Brazilian waved him off.
Pereira knocked Hill out cold with a brutal left hand and finished the job with some ground and pound.
Joe Rogan said of Pereira: “What a killer.”
MMA journalist Amy Kaplan wrote: “THAT WAS THE COLDEST THING I HAVE EVER SEEN.”
News Corp’s Brendan Bradford wrote: “Cold from Poatan. Wow.”
Ariel Helwani wrote: “Alex Pereira KOs Hill in the first round. Never in doubt for Pereira. And still light heavyweight champion.”
Barstool Sports’ Robbie Fox wrote: “Holy shit. Alex Pereira waves off Herb Dean trying to stop the action after a low blow, and puts Jamahal Hill down with the next shot he threw. Insane ending to an ALL TIME card.”
Former UFC fighter Kenny Florian: “Waves off cup kick, proceeds to use murderous left-hand. Alex Pereira ladies and gentlemen. Absolute savage.”
Israel Adesanya and Ilia Topuria couldn’t believe their eyes watching Max Holloway’s knockout ringside at Las Vegas.
Adesanya looked genuinely in awe, while Topuria’s facial expression after realising he would have to fight Holloway next was priceless.
Topuria knocked out Australia’s Alex Volkanovski to claim the UFC featherweight belt earlier this year.
Zhang Weili has defended her strawweight title with a unanimous decision victory over Yan Xiaonan in the co-main event.
Weili had Xiaonan in trouble with a tight chokehold late in the first round and Xiaonan appeared to be unconscious at the end of the round.
But she somehow woke up and her corner roused her with ice and smelling salts to keep her in the contest. 
“In the corner the cut man said I can wake her up and he gave her some smelling salts,” Daniel Cormier said.
“Are you allowed to do that? I don’t think you’re allowed to do that,” Joe Rogan asked.
Max Holloway has proved he’s still got it, defeating Justin Gaethje via knockout in one of the most impressive victories ever seen in UFC.
Holloway had the better of the early rounds, breaking Gaethje’s nose just before the end of the first before accidentally poking his opponent in the eye twice in the middle rounds.
Gaethje bravely fought on and managed to knock down Holloway — the first time he’s ever been sent to the canvas in his career.
Holloway dominated the fight in a vintage performance and could have cruised to a win via decision — but he called for Gaethje to trade blows him late in the fifth and final round.
And with just one second left on the clock, Holloway unleashed a blistering barrage of combinations and knocked Gaethje out cold with a right hand.
It’s undoubtedly one of the greatest walk-off knockouts in UFC history and sets up Holloway for a clash with Dustin Poirier.
“An absolute stunner at UFC 300,” Jon Anik said in commentary.
“Oh my god, oh my god,” Joe Rogan exclaimed.
“Holy shit. Oh my god,” Daniel Cormier said.
Rogan added: “He called for it. He called for him to stand and trade. That’s a real warrior. In fight he’s winning. He could have been up four rounds. Oh my god.”
Not only did Holloway win the lightweight blockbuster, he will took the UFC’s BMF belt for the “baddest motherf***er” off Gaethje.
Viewers couldn’t believe what they witnessed from the 32-year-old Holloway.
News Corp’s Brendan Bradford wrote: “Give Max Holloway all the 300k bonuses. That was unbelievable.”
Bartstool Sports’ Robbie Fox wrote: “One of the greatest finishes I’ve ever seen. Max Holloway is a BAD MOTHERF***ER!!!”
Ariel Helwani wrote: “HOLY SHIT. That’s one of the greatest finishes ever. The legend of Max Blessed grows. WOW.”
Joe Rogan called it “the greatest knockout of all time.”
The blockbuster bout between Charles Oliveira and Arman Tsarukyan was stopped midway through the first round when Oliveira committed an “illegal” kick.
With Tsarukyan in the dominant position straddling Oliveira, the former lightweight champion lashed out with an upwards kick that collected Tsarukyan in the head.
Joe Rogan said in commentary: “That’s illegal. That up kick is illegal. Absolutely an illegal strike.”
Daniel Cormer said: “Luckily it hit his shoulder first because if that went straight to the face of Tsarukyan he might have went down.”
The referee told Oliveira: “He was grounded. Do not do that again.”
Oliveira wasn’t penalised a point on the scoreboards but after the doctor checked on Tsarukyan, he was allowed to resume his position on top of Oliveira.
The Brazilian nearly pulled off a great escape with some submission attempts late in rounds, but Tsarukyan showed off his grappling skills throughout to claim an upset victory in the lightweight title eliminator via split decision (28-29, 29-28 x 2).
Jiri Prochazka wants to face the winner of the main event between Pereira and Hill after defeating Aleksandr Rakic via a clinical TKO.
Prochazka was stopped by Pereira in the second round of their bout for the then vacant UFC light heavyweight title in November last year.
Jim Miller has been left bloodied by Bobby Green in their lightweight fight that turned into a three-round war on the undercard.
Green dominated the bout, winning via unanimous decision (30-27, 30-25 and 29-26) and nearly got the finish in the third round.
But Miller wouldn’t give in — the 40-year-old was left bloodied with a nasty deep gash opening up under his right eye.
Bravo, Jim Miller! 100,200,300! Incredible feat! As real as it gets! 
Respect Jim Miller.. you Legend 
Jalin Turner has lost to Renato Moicano via a second-round TKO after an embarrassing blunder.
Turner knocked Moicano to the canvas with a strong left hand and he thought the fight was over there and then. 
The American arguably should have finished the fight with some ground and pound, but Moicano got straight back to his feet.
Turner thought he’d secured a walk-off knockout, but Moicano fought back to win with some savage ground and pound.
One UFC fan wrote on X: “Jalin Turner just delivered one of the lowest IQ moments I’ve seen in a long time. Jesus f***ing christ.”
JALIN TURNER KNOCKS DOWM MOICANO AND THINKS THAT ITS OVER??? 
“He thought he had him and he was walking off, which is very interesting that he did that,” Joe Rogan said in commentary.
“That’s a problem. You try to create moments when in reality he should have tried to finish it,” added Daniel Cormier.
“Nobody can hurt me,” Moicano said.“I’m ‘Money f******* Moicano. I’m not going to stop until I have the belt. UFC, you need to respect me, I am the best in the business.”
Aljamain Sterling was getting booed before he produced one of the craziest throws the fight promotion has seen in recent years. 
Sterling’s tactics of taking the fight to the ground were not appreciated by the crowd, but fans changed their tune instantly when he picked up Calvin Kattar and buried him into the floor in the third round. 
Sterling’s tackle was described as a “powerbomb” by ESPN commentators. 
“Oh my,” Joe Rogan said in commentary as the stadium erupted.
All three judges scored the contest the same, awarding the former bantamweight champion a decision win courtesy of 30-27 scorecards.
Dominant performance in his featherweight debut ð
Deiveson Figueiredo has proved he is a legitimate threat in the bantamweight division with a vicious TKO stoppage of Cody Garbrandt. 
Figueiredo submitted Garbrandt with a rear-naked choke in the third round. 
He is now 2-0 since moving up to the 145lb division and a title shot may not be far away. 
Alex Pereira has shared details of his second insane body transformation in as many days for his octagon return.
The Brazilian bruiser had no issue making the 205lbs title fight limit, although he was one of the final fighters to tip the scales during Saturday’s official weigh-ins.
But he showed in Instagram on Saturday night he’s piled back on 27lbs — just hours before the Sin City extravaganza.
Former middleweight champion Pereira revealed he was weighing 232lbs after his rehydration protocol to his three million Instagram followers on Saturday, 
MMA fans were left stunned by his dramatic body transformation, with one saying: “Damn.”
Another said: “How TF did he make 185lbs?”
And another said: “He’s not losing.”
One remarked: “Can’t believe he was making 185lbs.”
Already being billed as the greatest card in UFC history, the stakes of Sunday’s stacked Las Vegas blockbuster were raised again after Dana White agreed to have $US300,000 ($A460,000) performance bonuses.
The huge monetary increase – which in Australian currency equates to almost $2 million – was one of several big moments at a press conference stacked with talent, 
While White usually hands out four $50K performance bonuses at events, he was asked early in proceedings by a journalist about a fight week suggestion from BMF champ Justin Gaethje that it be upped to $300,000.
“Done,” White said simply, while also agreeing to have fight legend Mark Coleman wrap the BMF title around whoever wins between Gaejthe and Max Holloway.
Alex Pereira def Jamahal Hill via KO (3:14, R1)
Zhang Weili def Yan Xiaonan via unanimous decision (49-45 x 5)
Max Holloway def Justin Gaethje via KO (4min59sec R5)
Arman Tsarukyan def Charles Oliveira via split decision (28-29, 29-28 x 2)
Bo Nickal def Cody Brundage via submission
Aljamain Sterling def Calvin Kattar via unanimous decision (30-27 x3)
Jiri Prochazka def Aleksandar Rakic via TKO
Kayla Harrison def Holly Holm via TKO (Round 2)
Diego Lopes def Sodiq Yusuff via TKO (Round 1)
Jessica Andrade def Marina Rodriguez via split decision (29-28 x 2, 28-29)
Bobby Green def Jim Miller via unanimous decision (30-27, 30-25 and 29-26)
Renato Moicano d Jalin Turner via TKO (Round 2)
Deiveson Figueiredo def Cody Garbrandt via TKO (Round 2)

title: UFC 300 delivers as Holloway stuns with ‘greatest KO of all time’, Pereira plans ‘unheard of’ move
subtitle: UFC 300 promised so much, with 12 current or former champions headlining the promotion’s historic card – and it most certainly delivered.
content:
UFC 300 promised so much, with 12 current or former champions headlining the promotion’s historic card – and it most certainly delivered.
A main card, which included what Joe Rogan described as the “greatest knockout of all time”, ended with another thunderous finish as Alex Pereira floored Jamahal Hill with a brutal straight left hand.
Referee Herb Dean had initially stepped in to give Pereira time to recover after what looked to be a low blow from Hill, only for the champion to wave him off and then rock the challenger before finishing him off with a relentless ground-and-pound.
With it, Pereira made a statement in his first light heavyweight title defence to cap off what were three incredible fights to close out the card.
“What a killer,” Rogan said of Pereira.
Pereira, who now has eight knockouts to his name, has rapidly risen up the ranks in the UFC since making his debut over two years ago, winning championships in two weight divisions.
So, what possibly could come next? Well, if you take Pereira’s post-fight comments seriously, it could be a shot at interim heavyweight champion Tom Aspinall in Brazil.
“I want to continue defending this belt,” he said.
“I’ve talked a lot about fighting in Brazil if I won this fight. I won this fight. I’m not hurt. Nothing happened. I know there needs to be a lot of promotion behind a fight but I want to have a fight at heavyweight.”
UFC Hall of Famer Daniel Cormier said it would be “nuts” to even watch that fight.
“Even if he attempts to do this it would be unheard of,” Cormier added.
Dana White confirmed earlier in the week that the fight of the night and performance of the night would receive $300,000 bonuses, although Pereira has competition in Max Holloway.
Fighting for ‘The Baddest Motherf***er’ belt, Holloway and Justin Gaethje decided to let loose in the final 10 seconds of their five-round war.
Holloway already looked set to take a unanimous decision victory at that point but the former featherweight champion left no doubt as 
Gaethje had done well to remain in the fight by that point after appearing to break his nose in the opening round, which was the first part of a championship triple-header at UFC 300.
Gaethje landed a leg kick early in the round but it was otherwise Holloway who had the better moments in the first round, mixing up jabs to the body and the head. 
Holloway landed two strong left hands early before getting through with a right that had Gaethje backing up.
The former featherweight champion then ended the round with a bang, landing a spinning kick that appeared to have broken Gaethje’s nose.
“It looks like that nose is destroyed,” Joe Rogan said in commentary.
As if that wasn’t enough, two accidental eye pokes from Holloway in the second round only made matters worse for Gaethje.
“Oh no, that’s the other eye now, oh my goodness,” Rogan added.
Holloway continued to land some heavy shots in the third round, including a big right hand and spinning leg kick, that had Gaethje briefly stumbling.
But Gaethje, despite all the adversity he faced early in the fight, found a way to drop Holloway in the fourth after landing a right hand on the temple as the pair traded blows on the inside.
With it, Gaethje scored the first knockdown of Holloway’s career and finished the round with plenty of momentum, although the former champion took it right back in the fifth.
Holloway rocked Gaethje with a lethal combination and then backed him up against the cage, even mixing in a spinning kick at one point.
But still Gaethje stood tall, right up until the final second, when Holloway sent him crumbling to the canvas in emphatic fashion.
“An absolute stunner,” Rogan said.
“... That is the greatest knockout of all time, with so many people counting him out.”
Now, having once looked out of the featherweight title picture following a third loss to Alexander Volkanovski, Holloway is firmly back in the frame for a shot at champion Ilia Topuria. 
The unbelievable moments kept coming as Yan Xiaonan was just saved by the bell in the opening round of her unanimous decision defeat to champion Zhang Weili in their women’s strawweight fight.
Yan looked to be in serious trouble as Zhang locked in an incredibly tight rear-naked choke with 15 seconds left in the first round.
“This is incredible,” Rogan said in commentary as Yan appeared to be on the brink of passing out, with the bell sounded as the challenger stumbled to her feet, hardly looking in any position to continue.
“She was out,” Rogan added.
Yan continued to prove her toughness in the second round too as Zhang immediately went to take the fight to the ground once more, doing so successfully and brutally unleashing ground-and-pound punches.
Once again it looked like the fight was on the brink of being finished but Yan was able to survive the onslaught before finding herself in peril again, this time as Zhang locked in a tight arm triangle choke.
Yan though once again was able to eventually escape and then opened the third round by dropping the champion and went on to score two takedowns to flip the fight on its head.
The challenger carried that dominance in the fourth too, dropping Zhang again, this time with a perfect counter as she followed up a right hand with a perfectly timed left hook.
Zhang responded by scoring a takedown and went back to the ground-and-pound, maintaining back control for a good few minutes while inflicting enough damage to leave her challenger’s eye damaged.
The champion was able to take Yan to the mat again in the fifth round, doing enough to defend her belt.
Earlier, Bo Nickal opened the main card with his third straight submission victory to improve to 3-0 in the promotion, although he wasn’t too happy with his performance.
After submitting Cody Brundage with a second-round rear-naked choke, Nickal booed himself and gave two thumbs down, later telling Joe Rogan he was “a little bit embarrassed” with his performance.
“I expect to go out and completely dominate and shut that guy out,” Nickal said.
“... But at the same time I am also grateful I got to get past the first round... I have that experience under my belt now.”
Brundage was aggressive early, pressuring Nickal to try make his undefeated rival uncomfortable and even flashing an unsuccessful flying knee and spinning back fist in the opening minute.
It was an energetic opening from Brundage but that quickly came to an end as Nickal took him to the ground and maintained back control for the majority of the opening round.
Nickal initially went for the rear-naked choke but wasn’t able to get his arm under the neck of Brundage and that remained the case for the rest of the round, with the veteran never really looking in trouble.
Before Sunday Nickal’s longest fight in this MMA career had been two minutes and 54 seconds, although being taken into the deep waters wasn’t necessarily going to be such a bad thing for his development.
Although again Brundage found himself on the ground early in the second round and while he was surviving once more, Nickal was still doing enough to cruise his way to victory unless something dramatically changed. 
Unfortunately for Brundage that was not the case as Nickal eventually was able to get the submission and move to 6-0 in the process.
Elsewhere, Arman Tsarukyan outlasted current No. 1 ranked contender Charles Oliveira in an epic lightweight bout by split decision (29-28 x2, 28-29).
Oliveira, one of the best submission artists in the UFC’s history, looked to have secured an early finish in the opening round when he locked in a deep guillotine.
Tsarukyan though kept composed and was able to somehow escape before then rolling Oliveira over and getting on top in the guard of the Brazilian, flipping the script after he found himself in danger early in the round. 
There was some controversy too in the first round as Oliveira was warned over an illegal strike off the ground which Daniel Cormier said in commentary could have caused much more damage than it did in the end.
“Luckily it hit his shoulder first because if that went straight to the face he may have gone down,” Cormier said. 
Oliveira was not deducted points for the move as the fight then went to the second round, with the former lightweight champion opening the second by landing a big right hand.
Tsarukyan though ate it right up and then later fired a brutal axe kick before later landing a takedown as the fight once again became an intriguing ground battle.
Oliveira was largely able to defend himself before Tsarukyan landed some brutal elbows that spilt his rival open, although the Brazilian lasted until the bell as the fight went to a third and final round.
There was late drama as Oliveira locked in a D’arce choke with 20 seconds left but once again Tsaruykan remained unfazed as he waited it out, surviving to take the victory.
“I want to fight for the title next,” Tsaruykan said.
Elsewhere, the preliminary card opened up with one of the UFC’s fast-rising stars and a debutant making big statements.
First up, Diego Lopes made it three finishes in a row as he knocked out Sodiq Yussuf in the opening round of their featherweight bout.
Then, two-time Olympic gold medal winning judoka Kayla Harrison celebrated her UFC debut in style as she submitted former women’s bantamweight champion and No. 5 ranked contender Holly Holm in the second round of their fight.
Harrison told Rogan in her post-fight interview that she wants a title shot against Raquel Pennington next, declaring she will be a UFC champion by the end of the year.
“That’s what I came over here for. One thing and one thing only,” she added.
Meanwhile, Lopes called out Movsar Evloev, who he lost to in his UFC debut last year after taking the fight on short notice, but also welcomed a shot at the featherweight champion.
“If he doesn’t want to fight with me then I’ll go to Spain for Ilia Topuria,” Lopes said.
Later fights on the preliminary card saw former bantamweight champion Aljamain Sterling score a dominant victory in his UFC debut at 145 pounds, taking care of business against featherweight contender Calvin Kattar in a unanimous decision win.
The fight failed to deliver any real fireworks, although the same couldn’t be said for Jiri Prochazka’s TKO victory over Aleksandar Rakic.
The former UFC light heavyweight champion and No. 2 ranked contender looked compromised in the opening round after struggling to check constant leg kicks from Rakic, although Prochazka only increased his aggression in the second to hunt the finish.
Prochazka got just that, blitzing Rakic to force Herb Dean to step in and call an end to the fight.
Speaking after the victory, Prochazka said he wants a shot at the win of the Pereira vs Hill main event.
Prochazka was stopped by Pereira in the second round of their bout for the then vacant UFC light heavyweight title in November last year.
Elsewhere, Jalin Turner suffered a second-round TKO defeat against Renato Moicano after a humiliating blunder. 
Turner looked to be on top early, hurting Moicano twice with his pinpoint striking before appearing to put his rival on the brink of defeat with a brutal straight left hand.
It sent Moicano crashing to the canvas, seemingly gifting Turner the win should the American had immediately capitalised on the opportunity to finish him via ground and pound.
Instead, Turner appeared to believe he had scored a walk-off knockout and it proved a big mistake as Moicano recovered to get back on his feet and continue the fight. 
“He thought he had him and he was walking off, which is very interesting that he did that,” Joe Rogan said in commentary.
“That’s a problem. You try to create moments when in reality he should have tried to finish it,” added Daniel Cormier.
JALIN TURNER KNOCKS DOWM MOICANO AND THINKS THAT ITS OVER??? 
Moicano eventually got the finish as he showed Turner how to do it the right way, sealing the victory with a vicious ground and pound after taking him to the canvas.
“Nobody can hurt me,” Moicano said. 
“I’m ‘Money f******* Moicano. I’m not going to stop until I have the belt. UFC, you need to respect me, I am the best in the business.”
Earlier, the historic card kicked off in bloody fashion as veteran lightweight Bobby Green outlasted Jim Miller in a brutal three-round war on Sunday. 
Green dominated the fight on the way to a unanimous decision victory, with the scorecards reading 30-27, 30-25 and 29-26 and almost got the finish in the third round. 
On that occasion he knocked Miller to the canvas but the 40-year-old just refused to give in, making it all the way to the final bell.
It was a long-awaited fight between the pair after a knee injury saw their first scheduled bout in 2014 fall through, while then seven years later Green was set to step in at short-notice to fight Miller but collapsed after the weigh-in.
Then in 2022, when the pair were finally set to face off in the octagon, Green failed a drug test. 
Green said Miller’s comments earlier in the week suggesting he was to blame for the cancellation of their multiple fights was a source of motivation for him on Sunday.
“It wasn’t nothing personal for me... I’m just going to hit him a lot. For everything you were saying, I’m going to hit you a lot,” Green told the UFC.
“He was cut up, he was busted up, he was bloody and I told you, ‘I’m going to hit you a lot’. I think I got the record right now for the most amount of hits in my division and I keep climbing it and climbing it.”
With the win at UFC 300, Green rebounded from a savage knockout loss to Jalin Turner in December and now turns his attention towards a potential fight with Paddy Pimblett.
Why? 
“Paddy said my name and anybody who has ever said my name, I will find you... you say my name and I shall appear,” Green said.
“In his backyard, with his people, with all the opportunity in his favour and watch what I do.”
Alex Pereira (c) def. Jamahal Hill via KO in Round 1 — for light heavyweight title
Weili Zhang (c) def. Yan Xiaonan via UD (49-45 x3) — for women’s strawweight title
Max Holloway def. Justin Gaethje (c) via knockout in Round 5 — for BMF title
Arman Tasrukyan def. Charles Oliveira via SD (29-28 x2, 28-29) — lightweight
Bo Nickal def. Cody Brundage via submission in Round 2 — middleweight
Jiri Prochazka def. Aleksandar Rakic via TKO in Round 2 — light heavyweight
Aljamain Sterling def. Calvin Kattar via UD (30-27 x3) — featherweight
Kayla Harrison def. Holly Holm via submission in Round 2 — women’s bantamweight
Diego Lopes def. Sodiq Yusuff via TKO in Round 1 — featherweight
Renato Moicano def. Jalin Turner via TKO in Round 2
Jessica Andrade def. Mariana Rodriguez via SD (29-28 x 2, 28-29)
Bobby Green def. Jim Miller via UD (30-27, 29-26, 30-25)
Deiveson Figueiredo def. Cody Garbrandt via submission in Round 2

en_title: UFC 300 delivers as Holloway stuns with ‘greatest KO of all time’, Pereira plans ‘unheard of’ move
en_content:
UFC 300 promised so much, with 12 current or former champions headlining the promotion’s historic card – and it most certainly delivered.
A main card, which included what Joe Rogan described as the “greatest knockout of all time”, ended with another thunderous finish as Alex Pereira floored Jamahal Hill with a brutal straight left hand.
Referee Herb Dean had initially stepped in to give Pereira time to recover after what looked to be a low blow from Hill, only for the champion to wave him off and then rock the challenger before finishing him off with a relentless ground-and-pound.
With it, Pereira made a statement in his first light heavyweight title defence to cap off what were three incredible fights to close out the card.
“What a killer,” Rogan said of Pereira.
Pereira, who now has eight knockouts to his name, has rapidly risen up the ranks in the UFC since making his debut over two years ago, winning championships in two weight divisions.
So, what possibly could come next? Well, if you take Pereira’s post-fight comments seriously, it could be a shot at interim heavyweight champion Tom Aspinall in Brazil.
“I want to continue defending this belt,” he said.
“I’ve talked a lot about fighting in Brazil if I won this fight. I won this fight. I’m not hurt. Nothing happened. I know there needs to be a lot of promotion behind a fight but I want to have a fight at heavyweight.”
UFC Hall of Famer Daniel Cormier said it would be “nuts” to even watch that fight.
“Even if he attempts to do this it would be unheard of,” Cormier added.
Dana White confirmed earlier in the week that the fight of the night and performance of the night would receive $300,000 bonuses, although Pereira has competition in Max Holloway.
Fighting for ‘The Baddest Motherf***er’ belt, Holloway and Justin Gaethje decided to let loose in the final 10 seconds of their five-round war.

title: Woman charged after Australian bulldog allegedly thrown off cliff near Wollongong
subtitle: A woman has been charged with animal cruelty offences after allegedly throwing a pet dog off a cliff.
content:
A woman has been charged after allegedly throwing a dog off a cliff south of Sydney, following a lengthy investigation by police.
The 32-year-old was arrested at a Horsley home in Wollongong on Friday morning and bailed after investigators charged her with animal cruelty offences after the animal was euthanised. 
The move followed a more than year-long investigation after a bushwalker heard a dog yelping near Clive Bissell Dr at Mount Ousley in January last year. 
Searching around, he discovered a kennel tipped upside down at the base of an escarpment with an injured Australian Bulldog inside about 8.30am. 
The find prompted a rescue operation involving police and SES volunteers, but the dog was later euthanised due to his poor condition and injuries. 
Police will allege the kennel was thrown from the cliff overnight on January 22, 2023.
Investigators began a forensic examination of the kennel and arrested the woman following a “extensive investigation”, a NSW Police spokesman said. 
She is expected to appear in the Wollongong Local Court in May.

title: Conor McGregor’s long-awaited UFC return locked in
subtitle: Conor McGregor is officially back. UFC president Dana White revealed McGregor’s opponent and a blockbuster title fight for later this year.
content:
Conor McGregor is officially back.
Speaking after 
The bout will take place at T Mobile Arena in Las Vegas.
It will be fought at 170 pounds.
After a huge night of fights, White also announced UFC lightweight champ Islam Makhachev will defend against Dustin Poirier at UFC 302 in New Jersey in June.
That card will feature Sean Strickland versus Paulo Costa as the co-main, which will also be over five rounds.
Asked if McGregor’s hyped comeback fight had been difficult to finalise, White said: “It’s never hard with Conor.
“I say it all the time, Conor has always been easy to deal with. He’s a smart dude.
“Conor is a diehard UFC guy and will be until he retired and beyond.”
Throughout the week, whispers abounded that the UFC was planning a specific McGregor announcement during the Pay-Per-View portion of UFC 300.
That never happened however, with White simply handed a slip of paper midway through the press conference by a staffer prompting him to announce the McGregor fight among others.
Quizzed afterwards if a specific advertisement had been discussed for the UFC broadcast, the UFC boss simply smiled and said: “That’s all internet bulls…”.
It comes after McGregor doubled down earlier in the day on his return to the octagon befpre the official announcement after UFC 300.
McGregor, who has long been slated for a bout against Chandler, appeared to confirm via Instagram he would face the former Bellator champ on June 29.
The Irishman wrote: “THE FIGHT IS ON! MCGREGOR VS CHANDLER! See you on the date I said.”
The caption was accompanied with a picture of McGregor facing off against a man dressed in a yellow Teletubby suit as opposed to Chandler himself.

The caption was accompanied with a picture of McGregor facing off against a man dressed in a yellow Teletubby suit as opposed to Chandler himself.
Originally published as

title: Aussie boss admits to crazy WFH policy
subtitle: An Aussie boss has revealed that he encourages all his staff to work from home every day — but this policy comes with a massive catch.
content:
An Aussie boss has revealed he encourages all his staff to work from home every day — but this policy comes with a massive catch. 
Greg Weiss is from Sydney and has five full-time staff and several part-time staff working across his two recruiting businesses, Soulidify and Career365. Some staff are based overseas. 
A self-described “modern baby boomer”, before the Covid-19 pandemic and therefore well ahead of the trend, Mr Weiss adopted working from home policies for his staff,
Staff can work from the comfort of their own home permanently, as this keeps his overhead costs down because he only rents out a small office space. 
But it comes with a major caveat. 
If his workers’ productivity drops, Mr Weiss will monitor their screens.
He has also implemented other measures to keep employees on their toes, such as scheduling regular meetings and making them write up daily reports to show how productive they’ve been.
Mr Weiss said he made this clear to staff if they chose to work from home. 
He says the micromanager style weeds out workers who aren’t the right fit. 
“I go into the office two or three days a week, for my own sanity, but I’m a firm believer in remote work, as long as there’s accountability,” he told the AFR. 
It comes as Australians were rocked last year from the news that a company had 
Suzie Cheikho, 38, a former consultant at Insurance Australia Group (IAG), found herself in a media storm last year after the Fair Work Commission deemed that it was fair enough for her to get the sack, as she wasn’t doing her job properly. 
Ms Cheikho was sacked in February 2023 for missing deadlines and meetings, being absent and uncontactable, and failing to complete a task.
She had received a formal warning in November 2022 about her output and was put on a performance improvement plan. Ms Cheikho was subject to a detailed review of cyber activity, which analysed the number of times she physically pressed her keyboard on 49 working days from October to December.
The Commission heard the review found she had “very low keystroke activity”, averaging 54 strokes per hour over the duration of her surveillance, which showed “she was not presenting for work and performing work as required”.
Ms Cheikho, who told her employer she did “not believe for a minute” the data was true, took her case to the Fair Work Commission (FWC). The tribunal this week sided with IAG, finding she was fired for a “valid reason of misconduct”.
The Fair Work Commission is currently investigating whether working-from-home rights should be incorporated into industrial awards.
About 2.2 million workers on award wages could be impacted by the findings of the review, which will make recommendations to the federal government.
Workplace Minister Tony Burke said there were plenty of examples where working from home brought “mutual benefit” to both workers and employers and urged businesses to be open to discussions.
“It makes sense that the Fair Work Commission is saying okay, let’s check and work through submissions and see how that fits through the awards system,” he said.
Quizzed about the possibility of new working from home laws, Prime Minister Anthony Albanese said remote working was a very positive development to come out of Covid-19 particularly for working mothers – but warned against workplaces using a “one size fits all” approach.
“I think that it is important, from at least time to time that people gather in one place so they can have that social interaction,” he said.
Opposition Leader Peter Dutton said while he supported flexibility for workers, he held concerns about the impact decreased productivity would have on the economy.

title: Star’s Sydney executives to face grilling as second Bell inquiry commences
subtitle: Should the inquiry find Star is unsuitable to retain its licence, thousands of jobs will be at risk.
content:
A fresh 15-week probe into Star Entertainment’s flagship Sydney casino will commence on Monday with a host of former and current senior executives set to give evidence. 
Just 18 months since Star was fined $100 million after being found unsuitable to retain its casino licence, the state’s gambling regulator — the NSW Independent Casino Commission — has again tasked Adam Bell SC to conduct a review amid concerns that the company has not done enough to rectify problems identified in the original probe.
Should the inquiry find that the ASX-listed casino operator is again unfit to retain its licence at its Pyrmont-based casino, more than 3000 jobs and billions of dollars that the NSW government raises in revenue from gambling taxes will be put at risk. 
Among the current and former senior executives of the embattled gaming giant set to appear is government-appointed special manager Nicholas Weeks who is currently being paid almost $1 million a year to oversee Star’s Sydney operations.
Former chief financial officer, Christina Katsibouba, and former chief customer and product officer, George Hughes, are also slated to appear. The pair, who were among the few executives who continued to hold positions at the company firm after the first inquiry, quit in March.
Other witnesses include include ex-chief legal officer Betty Ivanoff, former company secretary Nawal Silfani, while current executives including patron liaison manager Ron Wagemans, head of risk Eileen Vuong, and head of gaming Peter Humphreys will also appear.
Meanwhile former chief executive Robbie Cooke, who also spectacularly quit the business in March after he and the board deemed it was in the company’s best interests for him to stand aside, is not scheduled to appear in the first week of hearings. 
Mr Cooke is expected to appear as a witness later this month, as will chair David Foster who is currently serving as chief executive until a permanent replacement is found. 
The probe follows a separate inquiry conducted by the NICC in 2022, also conducted by Mr Bell, which found The Star was unsuitable to retain its casino licence due to a litany of failures. 
Findings included that the gaming giant had not met its obligations to prevent money laundering and organised crime links.
“The cultural dysfunction had significant adverse consequences for Star Entertainment’s capacity to withstand the risks of criminal infiltration and money laundering,” Mr Bell’s 2022 report found.
While the NICC did not revoke Star’s licence, it instead appointed Mr Weeks as an external manager. Mr Week’s contract has been renewed several times and is set to conclude on June 30. 
According to the inquiry’s terms of reference, Mr Bell will probe whether The Star has, or is able to obtain the finances to ensure its continued viability, compliance with internal controls, and the culture within the organisation. 
The final report for the second Bell inquiry is due on May 31.

title: New mother Ash Good among six killed in Bondi Junction attack
subtitle: A new mother who had “the world at her feet” was among the six people fatally stabbed by a man at a Westfield on Saturday.
content:
A new mum who died after being stabbed during the Bondi Junction attack had shared a photo of her and her daughter just hours beforehand. 
Ash Good, 38, died in hospital on Saturday night, hours after she and her nine-month-old girl were stabbed by a 40-year-old man. 
Witnesses have recounted how she threw her injured baby girl, Harriet, into the arms of two strangers and begged them for help after being attacked by the lone assailant after 3.20pm. 
Two men, speaking to 9News, said she had handed her baby over to them as she fought for their life. 
“She handed us the baby and said ‘please help, help’,” one of the men said.
The pair said they used clothes to compress the bleeding while they awaited paramedics. 
Ms Good was taken to St Vincent’s Hospital in a critical condition, but she succumbed to her injuries not long after.
Harriet, meanwhile, underwent surgery and remains in a serious condition at the Royal Children’s Hospital. 
Just hours before the attack, she posted a photo of her and her daughter on social media, capturing the post “9 months out vs. 9 months in etc etc”. 
She had also shared a short clip of Harriet to her Instagram story, accompanied by the song “My Girl”. 
Sky News anchor Laura Jayes, who was a friend of Ms Good’s, described her as a “beautiful, beautiful girl, inside and out”. 
“She was so smart, and an athlete, and so clever,” Jayes said. 
“She saved her baby tonight.”
Reporting on her friend’s death live on air earlier in the evening, Jayes was emotional as she broke “the worst possible news”.
“I didn’t know the others but I knew one,” she said.
“She had the world at her feet.
“Her baby went into surgery and her mum didn’t make it.
“She was so smart, so beautiful and it was all ripped away in seconds.”Another friend took to Facebook to remember her as a “beautiful soul has been taken from us. 
“My prays go out to your child and family. You will be greatly missed,” he wrote, with a broken heart emoji.

title: Acting NSW Premier Penny Sharpe vows information on ‘beyond distressing’ Bondi attack to be made public
subtitle: Acting NSW Premier Penny Sharpe has vowed “as much” information on the fatal stabbing rampage at Westfield Bondi will be made public “as soon as we can”.
content:
Acting NSW Premier Penny Sharpe has described Saturday’s mass stabbing attack at Bondi as “beyond distressing” and vowed as much information as possible would be shared with the public. 
A lone 40-year-old man armed with a knife killed five people and injured at least eight more when he began his rampage at Westfield Bondi Junction at 3.20pm on Saturday. 
The injured included a nine-month-old baby and her mother. 
The mother later died in hospital, taking the death toll up to six. 
Speaking outside Waverley Police Station on Saturday night, Ms Sharpe said she was “personally distressed” by the incident, and noted there were “more difficult days ahead” for those directly impacted by the attack, as well as the wider community. 
“Today, New South Wales has experienced a distressing and horrific crime right here in one of the most ordinary of places, innocent people. Have been attacked at the local shopping centre,” she said.
“People enjoying the first day of the school holidays with their kids, people doing their ordinary shopping and their clothes shopping, and the workers who serve all of them. They have witnessed something horrific. 
“But even worse, we have lost six innocent lives.”
Ms Sharpe shared her condolences to those affected by the horrific attack. 
“Tonight we hold in our hearts the families and the friends and those who have lost loved ones,” she said.
“We stand with those who are waiting to hear. For those that have been injured. And we really stand by all of the medical staff who are fighting to save their lives.”
Ms Sharpe said she had convened the crisis cabinet, and was, along with the Prime Minister, receiving regular briefings from NSW Police. 
Protocols to provide support to first responders had been activated, she said, and she vowed information would be regularly flowing to the public. 
“We will be providing as much information as we can as soon as we can,” she said.
Premier Chris Minns, who had just arrived in Tokyo for a planned family holiday, had “not even left the airport”, Ms Sharpe said, and would be returning imminently, with his arrival expected on Saturday morning. 
“I am horrified to hear about the events at Bondi Junction this afternoon,” Mr Minns said in a statement. 
“I am making immediate arrangements to return to Sydney. 
“I want to thank NSW Police, emergency services and first responders and the community for their bravery in the face of this shocking incident.”

title: King Charles III ‘horrified’; Prince William, Princess Catherine ‘shocked’ by fatal Bondi Junction attack
subtitle: The royals have taken to social media to express their ‘shock and sadness’ at the ‘senseless’ fatal Bondi Junction attacks.
content:
The Royal Family has responded to the deadly Bondi Junction attacks, praising the “heroic” first responders. 
In a statement released late on Saturday night, King Charles III said he and Queen Camilla were “utterly shocked and horrified to hear of the tragic stabbing incident in Bondi.”
“Our hearts go out to the families and loved ones of those who have been so brutally killed during such a senseless attack.
While details of these shocking circumstances are still emerging, our thoughts are also with those who were involved in the response, and we give thanks for the bravery of the first responders and emergency services.”
He signed off: “Charles R.”
Prince William and Princess Catherine, who have also stepped back from public engagements as the Princess of Wales undergoes cancer treatment, also released a short message of support on X.
“We are shocked and saddened by the terrible events in Sydney earlier today,” they said. 
“Our thoughts are with all those affected, including the loved ones of those lost and the heroic emergency responders who risked their own lives to save others. W & C.”
A lone 40-year-old man armed with a knife killed six people and injured at least eight more when he began his rampage at the busy shopping centre at 3.20pm on Saturday. 
The injured included a nine-month-old baby and her mother, Ash Good who died in hospital. 
Police are yet to ascertain a motive, but say terrorism appears unlikely.
A senior officer who took on the attacker by her self and shot him has been praised a hero who saved “countless” other lives. 
The world was quick to respond to the tragic news out of Sydney. 
New Zealand Prime Minister Christopher Luxon took to social media to praise the “exceptional courage” of NSW Police. 
“Australia is family. So all New Zealanders are thinking of those affected by the tragic events in Bondi, especially the family and friends of those killed today,” he wrote. 
“Exceptional courage from NSW Police, first responders and members of the public.”
US Ambassador Caroline Kennedy said: “We are deeply saddened to hear of the events at Westfield Bondi Junction in Sydney today.
”Our hearts go out to the families and friends of those who were lost, and wish the injured a speedy and complete recovery.”

title: Men who confronted Bondi Junction knifeman armed with bollards revealed
subtitle: Two brave members of the public who confronted the Bondi knifeman armed only with bollards have been identified.
content:
Two men who confronted the Bondi Junction knifeman armed only with bollards have been identified as two migrants from France.
Damien Guerot and Silas Despreaux were at Bondi Westfield to attend a gym when 40-year-old Joel Cauchi, wielding a knife, embarked on his rampage.
Footage that emerged in the wake of the attack showed Mr Guerot facing off against Cauchi at the top of an escalator. 
Mr Despreaux also confronted Cauchi with a bollard during the attack, and the two Frenchman are believed to have helped lead police officer Amy Scott to his location, at which point she shot him, ending his spree of violence.
“He’s OK, just shaken,” a friend told the newspaper of Mr Despreaux.
“He says he is no hero, he is just an ordinary guy.
“He isn’t able to talk about it much.”
In the footage that was widely shared after the attack, Mr Guerot could be seen fending off the kinfeman from the top of an escalator.
He was hailed as a hero on social media, where he was dubbed “Bollard Man”.
“Standing ovation for Bollard Man at the Bondi Westfield stabbing,” reporter Josh Butler wrote on X.
Six people were killed, as well as the attacker, with many more injured during a 
An alarm was heard blaring as the centre went into lockdown, with people seen fleeing.
In another horror incident, the suspect was seen walking towards a family.
Another man, believed to be a father, 
Instead of running away, the father protected his family by walking towards the attacker, who skipped off in a different direction. 
The horror unfolded at 4pm on Saturday afternoon when witnesses say a man began stabbing shoppers at random, including a woman and her nine-month-old baby, before being shot dead by police.
Panicked families have described running for their lives as tactical police stormed the shopping centre.
Police were seen battling to save victims’ lives across multiple stores, witnesses said, with blood splattered throughout the once bustling mall.
Footage and photos have been shared on social media showing bloodied people lying on the floor.
The rampage ended when the attacker was 
Assistant Commissioner Anthony Cook said a man walked into the shopping centre about 3pm and attacked a number of people.
He said a female police officer was directed by witnesses and confronted the offender on level five.
“As she continued to walk quickly behind him to catch up with him he turned and faced her and raised a knife. She discharged a firearm and that person is now deceased,” he said.
“I’m advised that there are five victims who are now deceased as a result of the actions of this offender.
“There are more than several other people who have been conveyed to hospital.”
The female police officer reportedly told the suspect: “put it down” and shot him in the chest when he continued to advance.
She then gave him CPR.

title: Shoppers are obsessed with Aldi’s latest Special Buys range
subtitle: Aldi’s latest Special Buys range has captured a lot of attention with shoppers waiting outside before the doors even opened in a bid to get a piece.
content:
Aldi’s latest Special Buys range has captured a lot of attention with shoppers waiting outside before the doors even opened in a bid to get a piece.
Aldi released a streetwear collection with the range boasting a total of 23 items, all under $20, landing in the middle aisle on April 13.
The
There is also a grey sweatshirt, navy hoodie and trackpants that come in grey or navy for $14.99.
Sneakers, socks, a bucket hat, beanie, slides, compact umbrella and a double-walled insulated mug, which has three designs, are all part of the range as well.
 is one of the Sydneysiders who was waiting outside of the Aldi at Broadway shopping centre.
He wasn’t the only eager customer waiting for the doors of the supermarket to open up with others standing around beside him.
Before the electronic shutter door was even all the way up, James was underneath and had snagged a basket and headed to the middle aisle.
“It’s on today at Aldi,” he said, as a large group of shoppers perused the range.
TikTok user 
“When these came out in the catalogue I thought it was an April fools joke. Lowkey want the tracksuit though,” one shopper said.
Another said: “People are actually buying these.”
Belinda Grice, Aldi Australia’s Buying Director for ALDImania, said when news of the range broke: “We have been offering our shoppers high-quality, affordable loungewear for years, but this is the first time we’ve brought our own brand of leisurewear to the middle aisles.
“Designed to celebrate and champion our shoppers, this new range is made specifically with Aldi budgets in mind, without compromising on quality or comfort.
“This unique collection that contains sustainably sourced or recycled materials, has been crafted to create a mix-and-match aesthetic, so customers can inject their own sense of styling around the range.”

title: ‘I’m happy to tell people, this is how I did it’: How Red Wiggle Caterina Mete is changing what a family looks like
subtitle: She is making history as the first pregnant Wiggle. Now Caterina Mete – AKA the Red Wiggle – reveals why she is choosing motherhood on her own terms via an anonymous donor.
content:
Before she became an official Wiggle sporting a red skivvy, Caterina Mete had already been part of the children’s entertainment juggernaut for 20 years as a dancer and choreographer. Now, she’s opening up about her most personal role yet: as a mother-to-be.
In a candid interview with Stellar’s podcast Something To Talk About, the 43-year-old explains why she’s choosing motherhood on her own terms, as she traces her deeply personal path to becoming pregnant with twins via IVF.
“I’m feeling pretty good at the moment. The first trimester, I felt a little bit nauseous and extremely tired. People kept telling me, ‘You’ll be fine in the second trimester, you get this burst of energy’, so I’m feeling really good. I occasionally get a bad back so I’ve got
a back brace I wear when that happens, and I’m starting to get the swollen hands and feet that everyone talks about. But other than that, I feel great.” 
“I’m a pretty open and honest person. So it was something that I was never ashamed about. I was quite happy to tell people this is how I did it and, by being honest, you realise a lot of people have done it this way, too. So I was more than happy to share and to tell people my story. And, through that, a lot of people have reached out to me and told me their story, too. There have been some amazing stories [about] what women and families go through to fall pregnant. It’s lovely to connect with people in that way.” 
“When I was about 37, I decided to start egg freezing. It was actually a friend of mine at the time who was like, ‘Have you ever thought of that?’ I hadn’t, really, and it’s only because she brought it that up I started to look into it. It’s quite an expensive process, but I thought, I’m just going to do it. The first egg retrieval, they managed to retrieve seven eggs, and I did it the next year and they only got one, and then I did it the following year and they got two. It was amazing to see how diminished my egg supply went in just two years. I say to people now: if I could go back, I would have started it earlier. I know not everybody can because it is expensive. Then, during Covid, as hard as it was for everybody, it was a time to reassess your life and see what you wanted to do. I was like, I think I’m ready to have children.”
“It was tough. You [have] moments when you cry. You’re like, I don’t have a partner, it’s just me, how am I going to do it? And then you just go, Stop. Just do it. What are you waiting for? I’m older. I can’t be waiting any longer because that’s just unfortunately how the body is. You’ve got a time limit. I came to terms with it and then, when I started, when I decided on the donor, then embryos were created. I was fortunate to get two embryos, which is pretty good because I only had nine eggs; the more eggs you have, the more chances of creating embryos. I had my first embryo transfer, and before I went into it, in my head I was like, this isn’t going to work. I just had this feeling. Then you wait 10 days, and the doctor rings and is like, ‘Congratulations.’ I was in shock; it worked. So you deal with that and you’re happy and you go through all of that emotion … and then at eight, nine weeks, I miscarried. It was a shock to the system because you hear about miscarriages but you don’t think that’s happening to you. That was hard to deal with, but it’s amazing how many women go through it and obviously they don’t like to talk about it because it’s so hard and horrible. I was like, I’ve only got one embryo left. Before I even had the transfer, I kept thinking, what happens if that doesn’t work? I started looking at other options because I was scared. But thankfully it worked. When I had the ultrasound and the doctor scanned one side then went over to the other side, I just went, ‘Oh my goodness, I think that’s a second one,’ and he’s like, ‘Congratulations.’ It was a real miracle to have two with my last embryo.” 
“It’s funny because I come from an Italian family – my mum, my aunties and uncles, they were born in a different country; they’re not used to [the idea of a donor]. And not one person said, ‘Who’s the father?’ Not one, and that really shocked me. As soon as I said, ‘I’m pregnant,’ they’re like, ‘Oh, that’s great.’ They didn’t ask anything, and I thought that was so interesting because they’re in their 70s, 80s. I thought, well, they’re understanding the different times now.”
“Look, I just ignore that because I know the truth. I quite happily will say this is the journey I went on and it involved a donor from my fertility clinic. That’s the honest truth. People like to speculate but there’s nothing to speculate about, because that’s what happened.” 
“It’s quite beautiful what The Wiggles bring to children, and it’s nice to be a part of that journey. I’m the first pregnant Wiggle, essentially. Obviously the boys have had babies, but it’s their wife or partner who’s been pregnant. [At] shows it’s announced at the start that I’m pregnant in case people didn’t know, and it’s nice that families can talk to their children and say, ‘Oh, she’s having a baby, like how Mummy was.’ It’s nice teaching children about that, too.” 
Originally published as

title: ‘Never seen so much blood’: Bondi Rescue star caught up in stabbing horror
subtitle: The TV star was shopping for a new bed one minute, and the next he was “trying to stem the bleeding” from victims of the Bondi attacks.
content:
TV star Andrew Reid has revealed he tried to save the lives of two women when the horrifying massacre at Bondi Westfield suddenly began to unfold around him.
According to the Bondi Rescue cast member, he was shopping in Myer for a new bed when a frantic retail worker suddenly announced that “people are being stabbed” and “the building is being evacuated’ on Saturday afternoon.
The next minute he was “trying to stem the bleeding” of victims who had been stabbed by an unidentified man who went on a rampage around 3.20pm.
Myer reportedly dropped its shutters to protect those inside when gunshots rang out and it was clear a major incident was taking place.
Reid said he then looked out across the shopping centre to the fourth floor below and spotted a woman lying on the ground in desperate need of help and so asked to be let out so he could provide first aid. 
“There was a lady … and there was just blood everywhere,” Reid, a father-of-three, told 9News.
“I realised I had to go and help because that’s what we’re trained to do as lifeguards.”
He and two other members of the public administered first aid to the woman, along with police officers.
“I have never seen so much blood … we were just looking for wounds, trying to stem the bleeding,’ Reid said.
“As I looked up there were multiple victims about 50m apart. The lady next to us was in a really bad way – there were others working on her.”
Six people were killed and several others injured – including a nine-month-old baby – when a knife-wielding attacker rampaged through a busy Sydney shopping centre on Saturday.
Police said multiple people were stabbed by the unidentified assailant, who was tracked down and shot dead by a policewoman who is being hailed as a national hero.
The incident occurred at the sprawling Westfield Bondi Junction mall complex, which was packed with thousands of Saturday afternoon shoppers.
Tactical police stormed the building going floor by floor to secure the scene. During this time, Reid said he had helped to stabilise the woman, then searched for anyone else who needed help. 
“I saw police needed help with the other lady so I started doing compressions on her. I don’t think she made it,’ Reid said.
“Then I went to help the next victim and saw an empty pram. I have three young kids and just though oh my God.
While Reid has been involved in countless rescues and witnessed tragic outcomes, he admitted he’s never experienced anything so traumatic.
“I have seen some pretty gnarly stuff after working for 20 years on the beach, but nothing like this,” he said.

title: Legend’s call out Luke Beveridge’s bizarre remarks as pressure mount
subtitle: Western Bulldogs coach Luke Beveridge has been put on blast by several legends of the game over questionable post-game comments.
content:
The knives are well and truly out for Western Bulldogs coach Luke Beveridge.
Beveridge attracted widespread condemnation from footy fans on Friday night after he elected to name running defender Bailey Dale as the starting substitute.
The call to put one of the club’s best playmakers as the sub didn’t sit well with footy fans as several greats of the game called out the under fire coach.
Port Adelaide premiership star Kane Cornes put Beveridge on blast over comments he made during his post game press conference.
“When I talk to our supporters I talk to them about the possibilities and even though we’re evolving and there are some changes within personnel at different times and the players themselves have created an internal pressure for spots,” Beveridge said.
“That’s all OK if you end up being a formidable team. Right now we’re not. But whether or not there is any pain as we evolve and move into the future, because it is all about the now and what’s up next and what’s up next year. History is history.”
Cornes didn’t hold back as he took aim at the under fire Bulldogs coach and delivered a brutal clip.
“I feel like I need a translator after listening to that,” Cornes said.
“I don’t understand what was said in that minute or so that we just heard from Luke Beveridge. 
“It has to be better because if I’m a Western Bulldogs fan, I’m going ‘what is going on with our side? How does the coach see us? Are we in it for the now, the future or the past?’
“Because he covered all three things without addressing any of them.
“Cleary there is a divide between coaching and list management because Bailey Dale has been signed for five years and he’s a sub. 
“Caleb Daniel is on big money, as you told us Damo (Damian Barrett), long term. Jack Macrae signed for long term.
“Is there a disconnect between coaching and list management? Is there a disconnect between where the board sees the club because they have said top four.
“Luke Beveridge continually hints this list isn’t good enough to compete. 
“But once again you’re playing against Essendon who didn’t play finals last year and are without their best forward, they’re missing a number of key position players out of the side.
“You’re in that game and you get blown away once again and you’re unable to halt momentum. 
“I thought, if I’m a Western Bulldogs fan, where does the coach see us. Are we in it for right 
now or do we need to go through a rebuild? I don’t see that as being a fact. Really highly confusing stuff once again from Luke Beveridge.”
The press conference remarks didn’t go down well with AFL legend Leigh Matthews who said the messaging from Beveridge could be causing confusion among the playing group.
“That’s the public narrative you do in the post-match press conference,” Matthews said on Channel 9’s Footy Furnace. 
“You always have to remind yourself as a coach, you’re talking to your players. You’re partly talking to your fans and the media but you’re mainly talking to your players. 
“And if you have mixed messages in that forum about why are we playing these next generation players instead of the Bailey Dale’s and Caleb Daniel’s, why are we doing that?
“Every player thinks ‘aren’t we trying to win this week?’. And the narrative about trying to win this week, the words are being said but I’m not sure whether the actions are following that.” 
Three-time premiership star Jimmy Bartel said the current list is good enough for the Dogs to be pushing for a deep September run.
“I’d be wanting to win now. You just look at who is running out there at the moment, they’ve got a player who wer’re talking about could be the greatest Bulldog ever in the Bont … capitalise on him now,” Bartel said.
“Libba is playing as good a footy as he’s ever played, he wouldn’t want to be thinking that you’re looking ahead to two years from now.
“You’ve got emerging generational talent in (Jamarra) Ugle-Hagan and (Sam) Darcy could be anything.
“You’ve got All-Australian ruckman in (Tim) English, All-Australian backs in (Bailey) Dale and (Caleb) Daniel.
“Why aren’t you trying to win the premiership this year?”
